# Preface {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8). Reorganised by a LLM-based AI tool (Codex/GPT-5). This report is a work-in-progress draft; many sections are still scaffolds and will be written by the authors.
:::

::: {.callout-warning title="Preliminary"}
All data, models, and findings reported here are **preliminary** and subject to change as the analysis develops and further data are received.
:::

## Who this report is for {.unnumbered}

_[Scaffold]_ This is the **technical report** — a detailed account of what we did, what we found, and how to reproduce it. It is aimed at researchers and methodologically-engaged practitioners. A **separate plain-language publication** will summarise the findings and their practical implications for families and educators; the executive summary and the evidence table that follow are written so that companion can lift them directly.

We assume a reader who is comfortable with frequentist statistics (p-values, confidence intervals) but may be newer to Bayesian methods. @sec-bridge is a self-contained guide to reading the Bayesian results.

## How the report is organised {.unnumbered}

_[Scaffold]_ The report now follows the shared study-report structure: front matter (preface, acknowledgements, summary, contents, glossary), then Part 1 for background and aims, Part 2 for methods, Part 3 for results, Part 4 for discussion, and appendices for supporting registers, reproducibility material, use of AI, and references.

## Causal-status labels {.unnumbered}

_[Scaffold]_ Each results section carries a label fixing how its estimates may be read:

- **`[CAUSAL]`** — identified by randomisation; may be read as an effect of the intervention.
- **`[ROBUSTNESS]`** — checks on whether the causal result survives alternative assumptions.
- **`[ASSOCIATION]`** — adjusted associations only; never to be read as one skill _driving_ another (see @sec-dag).

## Conventions {.unnumbered}

_[Scaffold]_ Estimates are reported as a posterior **median + 95% equal-tailed credible interval**, with a tail probability $P(\tau>0)$ for direction and $P(\text{benefit} \ge \delta)$ for a meaningful effect — **not** p-values. A **positive $\tau$ means the intervention helps**. Full detail in @sec-methods and `METHODS.md`.
